Hello, San Francisco.
So, when Walt Disney created Disneyland,
he wasn't just building a theme park. He
was building an experience designed for
humans of all ages. He created an
immersive environment for his beloved
stories and cartoon characters to jump
off at that screen and be experienced by
families firsthand. He wanted families
to be fully present, engulfed in the
magic that he was about to create at the
the park. Walt cared so much about this
experience that he even placed trash
cans only 30 ft apart from each other so
not even trash could ruin the experience
for another guest.
He believed in stories first systems
second. Instead of building a park
around existing off-the-shelf carnival
rides, Walt hired Imagineers to go into
all of the rides and the stories and the
experiences and make something that was
truly memorable for the guests that
would eventually ride them. Every ride,
every sound, and every smell was
meticulously crafted so that the guests
could have this magical moment and they
could truly experience the magic of
Disney. Each magical moment when you
combine it together of the sights and
the sounds would be something memorable
about the brand and the story that he
wanted them to hear. But we've all gone
to Disney and we've all experienced
that. We've told it for generations
after generations. It's something that
the brand has engraved into them.
This approach has been replicated by

## 5. 4 Types of CRM Software You Need to Know

If you're a scaling business, looking to
move your team off of countless
spreadsheets and softwares to a single
CRM platform, you've totally come to the
right place. I'm Mark from HubSpot and
today I'm going to show you how
HubSpot's CRM can help your business
grow efficiently. To sign up for
HubSpot's free CRM, click the link in
the description below. It only takes a
second and it's totally free for life.
Most scaling companies have several
tools, softwares, and ways to manage
customer data. But with one CRM acting
as your single source of truth, all of
your teams can look at the same customer
data in the same place in the same way.
Now, there are four main types of CRMs.
Let's quickly take a look at each one.
An operational CRM is here to help your
company streamline and simplify all of
its business processes. Think about
generating leads, converting them into
customers, providing world-class
customer service, and delighting them
throughout the entire process.
Analytical CRMs rely on data gathering
and analysis to help companies best
serve their customers. Think of things
like customer preferences, behaviors,
and contact information.
A collaborative CRM brings together all
of your departments, like sales,
marketing, service, and operations, to
help them work together to identify
customers' needs, wants, and
preferences.
Strategic CRMs are often lumped in with
collaborative CRMs cuz they share a lot
of the same features. But whereas
collaborative CRMs focus on the
short-term servicing of customers,
strategic CRMs focus on long-term
customer retention and loyalty.
Now, HubSpot is an operational,
analytical, collaborative, and strategic
CRM. Let me show you the features that
make it a super versatile, all-in-one
CRM platform that aligns your entire
company from marketing to ops. Let's
jump in and I'll show you how it works.
The cornerstone of HubSpot's CRM is a
contact. To get started, scroll up top
to contacts and select contacts from the
drop-down menu.
Here you'll be brought to a page where
all of the contacts are listed. You can
filter them by contact owner, the date
they were added or created, the last
activity date, the lead status, or a
bunch of other advanced filters that we
have. To look at a contact, you can
simply click on the contact name, and it
will bring you to a page with all of
that contact's information. You can take
a look at what's happened across sales,
marketing, or even customer service in
one centralized place. I can see that
Jesse is the chief operating officer. I
can take a look at this activity tab and
see all of the activity that has taken
place. It looks like there was a meeting
that was scheduled, and I can see who
was invited, who attended, and see how
long it lasted. I can even take a look
at the emails that I sent to make sure
they were opened, and if any links were
clicked on, etc. I can click an email to
get deeper detail and get a sense of
what's happening with this particular
customer. I can even reply, forward, or
even delete the email right from within
HubSpot. But, that's not all. If you
scroll up top, I can see if there's any
notes that were taken after a phone call
or a meeting. I can track all the emails
that were sent. Take a look at any calls
that were scheduled. It looks like I
left Jesse a voicemail. Any tasks
associated with this customer, and
finally, any meetings that have taken
place. In the right-hand column, you get
a sense of what's happening with this
customer. Any deals that we have going
on, any service tickets that they may
have submitted to your service team, any
payments that have been made,
attachments that you sent, and a lot of

## 9. AI Engine Optimization ｜ HubSpot Spotlight Fall 2025

AEO strategy shows you how to succeed in
AI engines. And it works like this.
HubSpot analyzes your existing content
and surfaces actionable insights using a
stoplight system. Green for ready,
yellow for improvement needed, red for
needs work. Click into any topic to see
why. Get specific recommendations based
on AEO best practices and apply changes
to your content. Then, AEO opportunities
identifies content gaps and shows
exactly what to create next, so you can
do things like generate optimized blog
posts instantly. If you want to see how
you're performing across major AI
platforms compared to your competitors,
you can check your brand sentiment
score. Plus, every recommendation you
get is rooted in your actual business
data and context from your ICPs,
products, and brand identity. This is
where AEO goes from challenge to
competitive edge for your team. Get
started today.

Close deals faster with AI powered CPQ,
the tool that speeds up configuration,
pricing, and quoting in one seamless
flow. Leverage AI to generate
professional quotes in minutes using
deal context to update cover letters,
business case summaries, and line items
for faster customization. Create
personalized proposals and cover letters
based on the deal. Approval workflows
highlight changes and route quotes
instantly, eliminating bottlenecks that
slow down deals. Accepted quotes
automatically trigger billing and
payment collection, creating a
frictionless buying experience from
quote to revenue. Track buyer engagement
in real time, so reps respond to
requests faster, and buyers get the
streamlined experience they expect.
Built directly into HubSpot's customer
platform, every quote updates your
pipeline and forecasts in real time so
you can close more deals. Time kills
deals. Beat the clock with AI powered
quotes in HubSpot.

>> I get to give Erica the first question,
which is, is my data used to train AI?
This is probably the number one question

it's coming from a couple different

they come to the table where someone has

somewhere in between. So, a couple of
things to note. At HubSpot, we do use
customer data to train our AI for a
couple of different purposes. The first
is for personalization. And if you sat
through any of the keynotes this
morning, you should kind of understand
at this point that you actually really
want that. If AI is supposed to be an
extension of your team and a team
member, you want to give it context. You
want it to be highly personalized to
your team. So our AI, while it looks at
your data, can understand, for example,
how can I make this email tagline sound
a lot more like your company? You heard
some of the customer stories with
Morehouse College and how they were able
to form a real brand voice and use that
and it saved them time and energy as
they move through things. That's the
power of AI being able to train off of
your data. Now, when that happens, it
happens within your account. It happens
on your business data and it never
leaves sort of that instance. It is
highly personalized to you and it's
never used to support anyone else. The
second way that we use data to train our
own internal AI models is with your
permission. when we train on data to
make features better for everyone else.
And this is where we look at things like
what is the average deal time that we
close deals within a specific industry
in this area. And we can surface

deal typically takes about 30 days in
your industry and segment to close. Here
are some of the things that you should
do. We do that on an aggregated basis.
We never do it in a way that identifies
your business intelligence. We never do
it in a way that is shared with any
other customer or that could be used as
a competitive advantage. For the lawyers
in the room, this is a trade secret. Um,
and we never violate trade secrets. So,
those are the two ways within HubSpot
that we use data to train AI. And in all
cases, it's really about adding value to
customers use of our products. Now, I'm

that's the sort of vertical SAS
application space. when you're using a

choice is going to be, that's kind of a

understand what the application is

to is by design designed to hoover up a
whole bunch of context and have any
number of use cases attached to that. In
that case, you do not want your
sensitive business data being sucked up
by a large language model. And so, you
want to look at the terms really
closely. And here's a pro tip. In a lot
of cases, if you're on an enterprise
license, they already understand this
and they push a lot of businesses to
that license to say, "Hey, we've built
in these protections, so you don't have
to worry about that." The tricky part
here is that the business models are
still settling. So there's sort of
proumer tiers, there's consumer tiers,
there's a lot of different tiers of
licenses that are still emerging among
LLMs. And so that's why I always advise,
you know, customers, vendors, whoever
we're talking to, look at a large
language model, experiment with it,
figure out if it works for you, but then
once you do, upgrade to an enterprise
license because you're going to be
guaranteed that protection that says,
I'm the type of user that's putting
sensitive business information into this
model. I'm putting trade secrets in. I
do not want that to be used by my
competitors or used to teach other
people how to compete with me. So that's
the distinction there. We're going to
talk a little bit more about how this
works when we actually use LLMs to power
our AI, which spoiler alert, they are
not allowed to train on your data. Um,
but Karen, do you want to talk about a
little bit about this in the MCP
connector context?
>> Yeah. Um, I'll add one thing kind of to
that too. Erica talked about the

We think of this proumer tier as um
consumer tiers. So like if you're in
chat GBT, the plus would be considered a
proumer tier. The important distinction
is their business tiers like team and
enterprise. They're designed to make
sure that data is never trained on. So
by default um there is no setting that
says turn off training. They're not
trained on. The proumer tiers are like
the plus ones. There is a data setting
and we just want people to be educated
about that as well. It's your choice to

just asked me about MCP. If you went to

He taught um he talked about the model

two of them. We have two different kinds
of MCP remote servers. Um today is the
GA I believe if some folks are in the
room of our MCP server and then we also
have an MCP server that powers the
connectors that we do for chat GP and
claude. One really important thing when
I was looking at the MCP servers is MC
by CP by the way is just a protocol. So
if you find people using MCP servers, if
you see other HubSpot ones um around the
internet, there are a lot. One of the
reasons it was important that we built
ours is because we could protect OH and
user permissions. So in our connector
there are two layers MCP which is how
the data will get used and then we also
have an OOTH layer. What that does is
it's kind of like a handshake and it
makes sure that your HubSpot data when
it's used with any of these connectors
has a handshake. It says I know who you
are and I know what permissions you're
allowed to see. And so those two
handinand make sure that we can always
protect MCP servers. Um that is not true
for any MCP server out there. It is just
a protocol. So just make sure you know
you know how that's being used and it
was one of the reasons it was so
important for us to write our own.

of the hourglass that's moving but it's

## 15. Adding HubSpot Videos in Your Content

HubSpot video makes it easy to add
videos across your content let's review
how you can add videos to an email
landing page website page blog posts and
knowledge base article starting with
emails in your HubSpot account navigate
to marketing then click email
click the name of the unpublished email
you would like to add a video to
in the sidebar editor click and drag the
video module into your email
under the video type section select
HubSpot video
click upload to upload a new video file
from your computer or select browse
videos to select an existing video from
your files tool
edit your video details by selecting
edit a right panel will appear so that
you can edit the video thumbnail title
description and meta tags
edit the style of your video by clicking
style options on the left
here you can edit the play button color
and play button scale below you can
select whether to show the video at
maximum width on mobile devices
determine your video's alignment
and customize your video padding
once you're finished click done
now let's review how to add a video to a
web or landing page
both page types follow the same process
once you've located your page
identify your page and click edit
in the sidebar editor click and drag the
video module into your page
under video type select HubSpot video
click upload or select browse videos to
select an existing video
use the left panel to select the maximum
size of your video edit your video
details and customize your style options
you can also add an in-video CTA or form
click Advanced to add features such as
Loop video mute by default autoplay or
hide controls
click apply changes once you're finished
finally let's review how to add a video
to a blog post or knowledge based
article
both follow a similar process once
you've located your page
find your post or article and select
edit
locate and select your Rich Text module
using your toolbar above click insert
select video and choose HubSpot video
in the right panel select an existing
video file or click upload to add a new
video
use the toolbar above to edit your
player color determine your video
alignment and customize your video size
and spacing
select the pencil icon to edit your
video details here you can add a CTA or
form click Advanced to edit your video
player settings or click edit video
details to edit your thumbnail title
description and meta tags
once you're finished click apply and
there you have it you can now add
HubSpot videos to your emails website
Pages landing pages blog posts and
knowledge-based articles check out the
link to learn more about HubSpot video

## 16. Ads Management Tutorial ｜ HubSpot Marketing Hub

Traditionally, marketers have struggled
to capture people's attention with ads
because they're interruptive. They're
not the content that your audience is
truly interested in. But, there's a
better way to connect with your target
audience without being disruptive. These
ads blend harmoniously with the content
ensuring a higher engagement and a
better return on investment. So, how
does it work in HubSpot? I'm so glad you
asked. To get started with the HubSpot
ads tool, start by navigating to the
marketing drop-down menu and then
selecting ads. This will redirect you
directly into your ads tool, but if you
haven't connected an ad account to
HubSpot yet, it's going to take you into
a connection flow like this one. So,
we'll start by clicking connect
accounts. And then, in this case, we're
going to select to create and connect a
Google Ads account, but just know that
if you're connecting a Facebook or
LinkedIn Ads account, the setup flow is
the exact same. The reason that I bring
up Google Ads though is because I have a
secret. Google and HubSpot have teamed
up and when you create a new Google
account using the create ad account
option here, you actually get a $500
credit applied automatically to your new
Google ad account. This is going to be
really game-changing if you're just
getting started in that channel and you
want to make sure that your advertising
budget is going that extra mile in
today's competitive landscape at the
same time as you're vetting if this is a
channel that your audience is really
engaging with you in. To get started,
click create ad account and this is
going to redirect you into Google.
You're going to enter your contact
information, your credit card
information, and then it's going to
redirect you back to HubSpot and then
the steps are going to be the exact same
as we'll cover in this video. I already
have a Google ad account though, so I'm
going to start by clicking connect
accounts and then Google Ads. This is
going to take us to an authentication
screen where we're going to select the
email address that's associated with our
Google Ads account. And then, it's going
to take you into a permissions screen
where essentially you're allowing the
creation of Google Ads to happen in
HubSpot as well as reporting and
tracking on any of the Google Ads that
you're creating outside of HubSpot. Once
you've read these terms and conditions,
just click allow and this is going to
take you into HubSpot. Now, any of the
ads accounts that are associated with
the email that you authenticated with
will be shown here. Just select the
checkbox next to the Google Ads accounts
that you do want to connect to HubSpot
and you'll see that auto tracking is
automatically enabled. Highly recommend
that you keep this setting on because
this is going to be the setting that
allows you to keep track of the ads that
your contacts are clicking and engaging
with. So, just make sure that this is on
if that aligns with your nurturing
strategies. Once you're done making your
selection, click connect and then this
will allow us to proceed in the setup
flow. So, in the bottom right, we'll
click next and this will take us to the

what we could do with the hubspot

so if i look back four months before we

## 29. Breeze Assistant ｜ HubSpot Spotlight Fall 2025

Meet Breeze Assistant, your AI companion
that knows your business and delivers
the help you need right when you need
it. It instantly helps with meeting
prep, content creation, and strategic
analysis. All powered by your actual CRM
data and business context. With file
upload, web search, and connections to
Google Workspace, Microsoft Office, and
Slack, plus its own mobile app, you get
AI help wherever you work, all
integrated within HubSpot. Need
specialized expertise? Create custom
assistants trained on your specific
processes and knowledge. Turn documents,
playbooks, and company knowledge into
specialized AI assistants that provide
expert guidance to your entire team.
From marketing strategies to sales
enablement, custom assistants learn from
what works and share it with your whole
team. Access both breeze assistant and
your custom assistants from one unified
interface within HubSpot. No switching
between tools. Give every team member an
AI assistant that knows your business
inside and out.
Experience Breeze Assistant today.

---

## 30. Breeze Customer Agent ｜ HubSpot Spotlight Fall 2025

Breeze customer agent is your 247 AI
concierge that helps you generate leads
and instantly resolves customer issues.
Customer agent naturally answers their
questions, qualifies their needs, books
sales meetings, and creates contacts in
your CRM in real time. Need instant
support? It quickly handles everything
from simple FAQs to tasks like password
resets and order tracking. Always
personalizing the experience using your
CRM data. No more balancing customers
between teams. Customer Agent
automatically understands if someone
needs sales help or service and
seamlessly handles both, providing a
smooth experience without dead ends. And
it gets results. Customer Agent resolves
over half of all conversations
automatically, meaning happier customers
and fewer waiting on hold. With built-in
dashboards, you see exactly how many
meetings have been booked, leads
generated, and how it's driving your
business forward. Best of all, Customer
Agent works everywhere your customers
already are. Chat, email, voice, or
social. It's got your back anytime,
anywhere. Go live in minutes and turn
questions into qualified leads. Deliver
amazing support and grow your business
effortlessly.

---

## 31. Breeze Data Agent ｜ HubSpot Spotlight Fall 2025

Data Agent makes customer research easy
and fast by delivering readytouse
insights to your CRM workflows and the
data studio. Ask any question about your
customers and the data agent does the
data work for you. It searches your CRM
calls, emails, documents, and the web to
find the answer. Reduce time spent on
manual research and gain instant access
to brand new insights into your
customers.
Data Agent can answer any question about
your customers and then provide the
answer in context in your CRM, the new
data studio, or in an automated
workflow. Get answers about recent
company news, competitors, custom buying
signals, and more, helping you know
exactly when and how to reach out.
Research that once took hours or wasn't
possible at all can now happen in
seconds or even be automated in a
workflow. No data team needed, no
complicated tools to learn. Let Data
Agent handle the heavy lifting and
deliver the insights your team needs to
grow faster.
Get started today.

## 32. Breeze Marketplace and Breeze Studio ｜ HubSpot Spotlight Fall 2025

Breeze brings you a unified AI
experience with powerful agents and
custom assistants that actually know
your business. Browse Breeze
Marketplace, your one-stop shop for AI
teammates across marketing, sales, and
service. Install any agent with just one
click. Then head to Breeze Studio to
tailor each agent for your specific
business needs. No coding required. Set
your brand voice, target audience, and
business rules in minutes. Watch your AI
team work 24/7, monitoring
opportunities, engaging prospects, and
handling routine tasks automatically.
Build agents that work exactly how you
need them to. Your processes, your
rules, your way of doing things. Get
more done with AI that learns as you
grow. Start building your AI team with
Breeze today.

to give you a demo of the content agent.
The content agent helps you scale your
content creation efforts with minimal
setup time by helping you create onbrand
high-quality landing pages, blog posts,
podcasts, and case studies. When you
first start creating a blog post, you
will immediately notice our new topic
ideation feature. Instead of just giving

suggestions along with an explanation of
why each topic would resonate with your
target audience. Once you select an idea

research, talking points, and the
content agent will seamlessly
incorporate the content from that
reference file into the generated blog
post. Now, we know that every good blog
post needs a good image. And thanks to
our new AI image generation model, every
photo we generate is now sharper and
more professional. And that means there
are fewer photos with extra body parts.
All of this content is great, but it has
to engage your audience and it has to be
consistent with your brand. Our new
brand identity model starts working
right away, taking into account your
products, customers, and services to
ensure that the text we generate truly
represent your
brand. We've also streamlined the
content agent user experience. Take
landing page creation for example. We've
simplified the process of selecting the
goal for your landing page, allowing you
to go from concept to a published page
in just a few minutes. Once you select
the goal, the content agent will go to
work optimizing every element of the
page for maximum conversion
potential. This simplified interface
lets you focus on what matters most,
creating high converting landing pages
without getting caught up in complex
decisions. The content agent can even
take care of internal linking for you.
Every blog post that you generate
includes a smart connection to your
existing content, enhancing both your
reader engagement and your SEO. Your
visitors will discover more relevant
content and search engines will better
understand the overall value of your
site, all without any extra effort on
your part. These improvements deliver
immediate impact, allowing you to focus
on creating highquality onbrand content
from day one with minimal setup and
maximum efficiency. Visit
hopspot.com/spotlight for all the latest

## 55. Customer Success Workspace： Never Lose Sight Of What Matters Most ｜ HubSpot Spotlight

The customer success workspace brings
everything together in one place,
flagging at risk accounts with real-time
health alerts the moment something
changes. Track customer health over time
with health scores that combine data
from across your business, engagement,
activity, support tickets, and even
product usage. See your entire book of
business at a glance with clear health
indicators so you can prioritize which
customers need attention right now,
helping managers spot trends and
allocate resources
effectively. Transform insights into
action with streamlined workflows for
reaching out, scheduling follow-ups, and
tracking customer requests. No more
jumping between systems or piecing
together customer context from scattered
data. The customer success workspace
gives your team everything they need to
react quickly to risks and proactively
drive growth. Whether you're managing 50
accounts or 5,000, visit
hubspot.com/spotlight to learn more.

## 57. Data Studio ｜ HubSpot Spotlight Fall 2025

Data Studio connects all your customer
data in seconds, knocking down data
silos and delivering unified insights
across the HubSpot platform. Easily add
data from external apps and your data
warehouse to the data studio. You can
even connect directly to spreadsheets in
Google Sheets and Microsoft Excel 365
files, updating data sets automatically
and eliminating manual exports, imports,
and other time-consuming data work.
Blend customer data from external
sources with data from your smart CRM
with the help of AI creating custom data
sets. Use data sets to power segments,
automation, and reports or choose to
sync your combined data into the smart
CRM. Spend minutes, not hours, on data
prep. Then keep your data clean with AI
powered tools in data hub that find and
fix problems before they impact your
business.
AI scans your database 24/7, merging
duplicates, fixing formatting, and
keeping your data clean. By proactively
spotting and filling missing contact
information, AI keeps your data
trustworthy and ready to use. Every
customer touch point feels personalized,
and your marketing dollars and your time
aren't wasted on messy data. With
better, faster data quality tools
accessible through a consolidated data
quality overview, you can proactively
monitor your CRM data health and be
alerted to potential issues before they
impact your customer experience. Let AI
keep your database clean while your team
focuses on growth.

---

## 60. Does Your Brand Show Up in AI Search？

Look, my life's a mess, like in every
area. But HubSpot AIO actually improved
one thing for me. Hello
Loverboy, a delicious better-for-you
canned cocktail. Shows up in AI
searches, which means I spend
less time spiraling and more time
convincing people I know how to DJ,
which I totally do.

that bet than there is right now. That's
because AI isn't replacing partner

to build, I think our approach as an

above all,
open by design, trusted by default.

type of platform we want to be and the
kind of ecosystem that will thrive in
the AI turn. So, what does that mean for
partners in our ecosystem? I see four
areas of opportunity.
The first is the mid-market. Large
mid-market deals grew north of 40% last
year, and HubSpot's agentic customer
platform is delivering on the depth and
context that drives outcomes. Second,
agentic acceleration.
AI-first engagements are growing at an
accelerated rate, and we're working
toward full API parity.
Anything you can do inside HubSpot,
you'll be able to do through our APIs,
MCP server, and CLI. Third, marketing
transformed. Loop marketing and AEO are
reimagining marketing services to focus
on ongoing outcome optimization.
The partners who run agentic demand
engines through the full loop will own
the most durable revenue relationships
in the ecosystem. And lastly,
specialized expertise. Regional
expertise or vertical, complex
industries, strict governance, security
compliance, these are all areas where
deep expertise wins. Across all four
categories, the data tells the same
story. The partners who move first will
define what customer value looks like in
the AI era. At HubSpot, we know that
when our partners thrive, our customers
win. And that's why we are all in for
our ecosystem.

## 83. Google Performance Max： Find More High-Value Customers With HubSpot

You know what I hear from a lot of
marketers? Their ads look good on paper,
great click-through, solid
conversions, but they still feel like
they're missing out on revenue. And
honestly, the problem usually isn't that
their campaign isn't working.
It's that they're not using their best
data to capture existing demand, leaving
countless new customers undiscovered.
Here's the thing. You're already using
your valuable first-party data from
HubSpot CRM to turn leads into customers
and grow your business. But what if you
can use that same intelligence to
proactively find your next best
customers over Google's entire network?
That's exactly what HubSpot and
Google have made possible by adding
performance max or PMAX as the new
campaign type that you can build
right inside of HubSpot.
PMAX is your customer discovery
engine. It's Google's all-in-one AI
powered campaign type that takes your
unique business data and uses it to help
you find converting customers across
YouTube, Gmail, Maps, Display, and more,
all in a single campaign. Here's what
makes this integration so powerful. It
connects Google's power to reach people
everywhere to what HubSpot already knows
about who your best customers actually
are, so that you're not just getting
more leads, you are getting the right
leads. You're essentially telling
Google, "Go find me prospects that are
like my best customers using the data
that users have actually agreed to share
with you." I'll walk you through exactly
how to set up a PMAX campaign within
HubSpot so that you can stop guessing
and start showing real revenue impact.
Make sure you click the link in the
description to follow along. Let's get
started. Step one is I build two lists
within HubSpot. One list of my highvalue
customers, not just the big spenders,
but those who convert quickly and
actually stay with us. Then I create a
list of lowquality leads, aka the people
who click our ads but never actually buy
anything. Once both of those lists are
created, I connect them both to my
Google PMX campaign through HubSpot's
ads tool. The result, PMAX didn't just
get more efficient. I started finding
highv value prospects in places I wasn't
even looking, like on their YouTube or
in their Gmail, while helping my sales
team focus on only the highest quality
leads. Now, getting the setup right is
only half the battle. Creative and
tracking makes all the difference. At
first, I was just guessing which ad copy
and images would get the most attention.
But clicks alone don't always tell the
whole story. They don't always lead to
the outcomes that you care about, like
demo bookings or qualified leads. Once I
connected conversion tracking in HubSpot
to those meaningful actions, I could see
which visuals and headlines actually
drove real results with the right
audiences. I used those insights to fuel
PMAX with my best performing creative,
and PMAX automatically handled the rest.
finding the right creative mix for each
audience on each channel and then
feeding those insights right back into
HubSpot so my sales team knows exactly
which leads to prioritize. If you're
like me, you want to monitor those
campaigns closely in the first few weeks
just to make sure everything's tracking
properly. But what I learned is to stop
getting caught up in the day-to-day
click data and start tracking the entire
customer journey. So instead of just
watching daily cost per click, I
actually set up reports within HubSpot
to track lead quality and how those
leads are actually moving through our
sales pipeline. That's when I saw the
real value. These leads were converting
to customers at almost double the rate
of our other channels. When you do the
math on lifetime value, PMAX was
actually our most profitable channel. So
what's the real takeaway? It's about
shifting from capturing demand to
proactively discovering it. You're
taking your rich HubSpot data and using
it to guide one single campaign that
reaches customers across Google's entire
ecosystem. Instead of just hoping for
the right audience, you're telling
Google's most powerful campaign engine
exactly who to find and then letting it
do the work. That's how you drive real
revenue impact at scale. If you're ready
to set this up, HubSpot's marketing
tools are free to get started. And
Google is offering new advertisers $500
in ad spend after you've spent
your first $500. Make sure you check out
the link in the description to try for
yourself. Thanks for watching and I'll
catch you in the next one.

pick one of these options that come in
and then uh chain it to send that gift.

end-to-end uh gift sending tool which

going to build an active list to see
which customers I've interacted with

we're going to give our list a name,
last contact 90 days.

we'll start to add some filters to see
who's going to make it on our list. The
first filter is I want to make sure the
contact's job title is either CEO or
COO. So, I'll go ahead and add in CEO,
COO, or I'll even add in chief operating
officer.
Here, you can start to see the list is
already previewing on the right-hand
side. Next, I want to add another filter
to see which contacts have not been
contacted in over 90 days. So, I'll
select the property, last contacted, is
down here is more than 90 days ago.

again, this is now showing on the left
that this group title will have anybody
whose job title is CEO, COO, or chief

contacted more than 90 days ago. Once

list. Now that we've created an active
list for prospects that we have not

## 120. How to Build a Commerce-Powered CRM with HubSpot

What's up, everybody? This is Ryan Gunn
from Aptitude 8. Today I'm going to talk
about how to make HubSpot a
commerce-powered CRM.
So the first thing that you need to
know, obviously, is what is a
commerce-powered CRM. So your customer
relationship management platform is the
go-to data source for your marketing,
your sales, and your customer success
team. But when you have back-office
commerce data like accounting payments
available in the CRM, those teams can
actually act on that data and do their
jobs better. That additional context can
fuel their activities, drive revenue
through customer retention,
faster payments, and even net new sales.
So how do you actually get commerce data
into your CRM? Depending on your
business's maturity and existing
commerce systems, there are three
potential ways in which you can get
payments and other back-office data into
HubSpot. If you're brand new to digital
commerce, then HubSpot's payments
feature is a great option for you. So
HubSpot offers a secure payment
processing solution that allows you to
accept payments directly through your
HubSpot account. That means you can
embed payment links in website pages,
emails, quotes,
or pretty much any other HubSpot asset.
And because it's native to the platform,
there's no need to integrate HubSpot
payments if you already use HubSpot CRM.
HubSpot Payments is an excellent option
for options that are just getting
started with digital commerce and want a
low-friction way to accept payments
online. But as your company gets deeper
into commerce, you're probably going to
want a more robust system that is more
customizable, scalable, and gives you
additional features like inventory
management, metered billing, payment
schedules, entitlements, tax automation.
If you already have a robust financial
infrastructure, then you're probably
looking at integrating an ERP. An ERP is
an enterprise resource planning system
and it's a one-stop shop for business
operations, including finance,
accounting, inventory, and more. Some
common examples of ERPs are NetSuite,
Sage, SAP, Microsoft Dynamics, and
Epicor. Because these are dedicated
systems outside of HubSpot, you'll need
to integrate them into your CRM. Some
ERPs are going to have a dedicated
integration that's already built out,
but some are not. And even the ones that
do may or may not offer the level of
functionality that you need from them.
So you're likely going to need some
developer help in order to really get
that integration customized and working
the way that you needed it to. If
digital commerce is core to your
business, then you probably already use
Stripe. Stripe is the number one secure
payment solution for companies that
accept payments online. It's got a
really well-documented API and knowledge
base, and it requires very little work
for you to integrate into your app or
your website. Stripe does have a native
HubSpot integration that will sync some
data into your CRM, but there's an even
more powerful integration that's already
in the HubSpot marketplace, and that's
called Zebra. Zebra is an app developed
by Happily that not only payments data,
but also allows you to integrate that
data seamlessly into reports and even
trigger automations based on payment
data. With HubSpot, Stripe, and Zebra
together, you can manage your
subscriptions, memberships, report on
things like monthly recurring revenue,
view payment information at the company
level, and more. And HubSpot allows you
to provide a better customer experience
than Stripe alone. So you're really
getting the best of both worlds when you
use Zebra to connect them. So what
happens when you don't have commerce
data already in your CRM?
When marketing, sales, and customer
success don't have access to payments
and other commerce data, then
client-facing members of your team are
interacting with your customers with an
incomplete picture of your relationship
with them. This can result in totally
avoidable situations like providing
services to customers that are behind on
payments or have expired payment
methods, attempted upsells to customers
who already have that service or product
or otherwise are not eligible for that
upsell, failing to treat big spender
customers like VIPs and and really
foster that relationship so it can
expand, or marketing irrelevant services
to your customers. Now I'm going to go
over how your go-to-market teams can
actually benefit from commerce data in
your CRM with some real-life use cases.
Let's start with the marketing team. So
while your marketing might not be the
first department you'd think would
benefit from payment data since they
primarily operate before a payment is
made, there are actually some
interesting use cases for marketing.
First is attribution. Marketing can use
payment information to close the loop on
sales activities that they have
influenced. By associating payments and
subscriptions to contact records,
marketers can more easily report on what
marketing activities led to revenue. A
report such as monthly recurring revenue
by original source can tell you what
channels are actually driving monthly
revenue. And you can even use custom
source properties like the ones that
we've mentioned in our attribution
videos. You can also look at monthly
recurring revenue from affiliates or
partners and look at what relationships
you've forged that are actually
generating revenue and which ones are a
waste of your time. And then you can
create reports to look at this
information over time so you can see how
that relationship changes or how
different channels are going up or down
in terms of their effectiveness. So if
you see here, we've got monthly
recurring revenue by original source
over time. And if we go to this organic
search, you can see there was kind of a
drop-off there. So what pages were
ranking that have suddenly dropped out
and we're no longer getting revenue from
those sources. Another excellent way
that marketers can use commerce data is
for list segmentation. Marketing teams
are often segmenting their audience so
they can more effectively target them
with relevant messaging, and commerce
data gives marketers even more options
for how to segment lists of contacts and
companies based on things like purchase
history and spending patterns and even
subscription status. So if we look here,
we've got this Zebra subscription is
equal to active and product is any of
keg. So we have a list of contacts here
that we know exactly what they've
purchased, and we can send them relevant
information potentially for an upsell.
The last way I'm going to talk about
today for marketers to use commerce data
is to trigger automations. Depending on
how you have your commerce system
integrated with HubSpot, you may be able
to trigger workflows based on payments
made or even missed, subscription status
changes, or time since their last
purchase. Using these triggers,
marketing teams can welcome new
customers, remarket to churned
customers, upsell to existing ones, or
reward loyal customers. This workflow is
an example of how to win back a customer
that has churned. So we've got the
subscription status is canceled. We're
going to wait for 30 days and then check
again, make sure they haven't renewed
their subscription. And if they haven't,
if they're still an inactive customer,
then we're going to send them this
win-back email that's going to give them
an offer or some sort of incentive to
come back and renew their subscription.
Next up, let's talk about sales. Sales
teams need insight into their customers'
purchasing behaviors and patterns, which
can help them improve their sales
strategies and close more deals. Here
are some ways sales teams can leverage
payments data inside their CRM. The
first way we're going to look at is
account-based sales. By viewing payment
data at the account level, such as with
a report on MRR by company, sales teams
can see penetration into their target
accounts. So you can see that here.
We've got our list of target accounts,
and then we can see how much MRR is at
each of those accounts. Account-level
data can also help sales teams
strategize by using purchase history to
inform upsells or expansion in accounts,
or by analyzing customer behavior like
seeing which products are most often
purchased together. You can also use
commerce data in your CRM to generate a
little friendly competition among your
sales reps. You can motivate your team
by creating a leaderboard to show which
reps have generated the most MRR, or you
can let reps see how much commission
they've generated in real time. Without
payment data in your CRM, commission
reporting can only be based on the
closed-won status of deals, which can
inflate numbers due to customers that
close but never actually pay. The last
and potentially most exciting way that
sales teams can use commerce data is for
forecasting. In order to set new goals
and create an effective sales strategy,
sales leaders not only need to know
about what new revenue is coming in, but
also when recurring revenue is churning.
By looking at the contract end date, you
can see how future recurring revenue may
drop off, and you can set quotas
accordingly to fill that gap and achieve
your company goals. The last team that
we're going to be talking about is
customer success, and this is where
having payments data within your CRM
really shines because it makes that
handoff between sales and customer
success way smoother. That is a huge
friction point for a lot of companies,
and it's way easier to automate that
process if you have payment data in your
CRM. So we're going to talk about how we
can facilitate that onboarding process.
That handoff between sales and CS is the
first impression you're going to make on
new clients, and you know, a lot of
times it's handled pretty clumsily. With
payment data integrated into your CRM,
you can automate your onboarding process
so that kicks off as soon as the first
payment is made. So here we have a
workflow to create an onboarding ticket
as soon as a subscription is started.
When that subscription status changes to
active, we create a new ticket and
assign it to a customer success rep.
Another way that you can use commerce
data is to celebrate relationship
milestones. Customer success is all
about ensuring that your clients have a
positive experience with your company.
And one way you can do that is to show
appreciation when your customers reach
certain milestones, such as expanding
their engagement with you or sticking
with you for over a year.
With commerce data in your CRM, you can
create automation that will send them a
thank you for being a great customer.
So I'm going to walk you through how we
would create that workflow.
If you go to your workflows, you can
create a new one from scratch.
In this case, I'm going to use a
Zebra-powered trigger, but if you have
an ERP integration or HubSpot Payments,
then there are other ways that you could
potentially work around to create
something similar.
The trigger criteria is going to be
based on the
create date.
Then we're going to delay for 365 days
to celebrate 1 year.
We're going to make sure that the
subscription is still active.
You're going to select the email that
you want to send and then hit save.
So this is what that ends up looking
like and then
when customers reach that milestone with
you, they get a nice thank you.
The last way that customer success teams
can use
commerce data is to automate account
health checks. So you can see here we've
got past due subscriptions. Manually
coming through accounts for missed
payments is pretty tedious
and it leads to unrealized revenue.
If your payment due dates and completion
data is in your CRM, you can create
workflows that recognize when a payment
is late and send an automated reminder.
So here's an example of that. If we have
a subscription status of past due,
we can wait 14 days. If it's still past
due, we will send a reminder email.
Incorporating commerce data into your
CRM is a vital step towards unlocking
the full potential of your CRM platform.
Whether you're using HubSpot payments,
an ERP integration, or Stripe and Zebra
together, your go-to-market teams can
make better informed decisions that can
fuel their activities and drive revenue
growth.
By investing in commerce-powered CRM,

experience and boosting your business's

## 136. HubSpot Operations Hub Starter Plan (Walkthrough)

As your company grows and scales, your
data might get more complicated. The
good news is Operations Hub Starter has
everything you need to keep your data
nice and clean. To get started, click
the link in the description below and
sign up for our free tools. Then, when
you're ready, you can upgrade to
Operations Hub Starter and keep access
to all the free tools. This way, your
data is tight and your operations are
locked in. To get started, let's take a
look at how the properties in your CRM
are performing. As a reminder,
properties are things like name, company
name, city, location of a contact. These
are all individual properties that you
can report on as data inside HubSpot.
So, here we can see that we have a total
of 403 properties and we can quickly see
if any of them have any issues, such as
not using any data, they're unused, or
they have duplicates. If you want to get
deeper to the insights, you can simply
click view all property insights and
here you'll be brought to a page that
actually shows you how the properties
are performing and any that you need to
review. As an example, here you can see
we have a property called about us. The
issue is that there is no data and it's
not being used. It lets you know what
type of an object that is a part of, is
it a ticket, a deal, or a company, or a
contact. It lets you know when the
property was updated, the source from
which the update came, and the percent
rate of it being filled, and the last
time it's being used in. You can see
we've got some properties that need some
attention here. Now, let's take a look
at your records. As a reminder, these
are companies or contact records inside
your CRM. Here we can again see that we
might have two main issues that might
occur with a record. There might be
formatting issues or duplicate issues.
Now, HubSpot uses HubSpot AI to detect
when duplicate records are being
created. For example, if you go to enter
somebody with the same first name and
last name, or the same email address,
HubSpot will flag it right away and say,
"Hey, are you sure you want to add this
person twice?" But, sometimes a unique
instance might come up where it might
get flagged over here in Operations Hub.
So, in this instance, I can choose to
either review or reject this duplicate.
If I click review, it's going to show us
the two records, all the information we
have gathered, and ask us which record
would you like to keep. It then will let
us know what happens when you merge the
contacts, and then you can go ahead and
merge the contacts. And just like that,
your data is nice and clean thanks to
Operations Hub. As your business grows,
your needs might become more complex,
and you might use some of our apps for
HubSpot, things like Aircall or other
sales tools. Now, these apps can connect
data back to your CRM, so you don't have
to go to a million places. And you can
check on the health of that data by
scrolling down to where it says data
sync. Here, it will let you know if you
have any apps with any sync failures or
apps with no active syncs. At any time,
if you want to see what kind of apps you
can add to HubSpot, simply scroll up top
to the app marketplace, select app
marketplace, and search for apps. For
example, Aircall is a great app that
many HubSpot customers use. Here, we can
go ahead and install the app, and right
away, it's going to tell us to connect
to our Aircall account and set up the
data sync right out of the box. At any
time, if you want to check on your
connected apps to see how the data is
syncing, you can scroll up top to the
app marketplace and scroll down to
connected apps. Here, you'll be brought
to a page that shows you all the apps
that are currently syncing data from the
app into HubSpot. Data is like the food
for your company. If you have junk food,
you're going to feel like junk. But if
you got good data, you'll feel great.
You can use Operations Hub to make sure
your data is clean and fueling your
business for growth. HubSpot is built to
grow with you. That's why we're the
leading choice among small business,
mid-market, and enterprise customers. To
get started, click the link in the
description below to sign up for
Operations Hub Starter. You can also
check out our Starter Customer Platform
Bundle. It's a great deal cuz it
includes Operations Hub Starter, Sales
Hub Starter, Marketing Hub Starter, and
so much more to help you grow your
business. As a note, HubSpot works to
make updates to our Customer Platform
every single day. We're even updating
our navigation bar in beta testing with
users as we speak. I'm Mark from
HubSpot. Thanks for watching.

it has completed the workflow that's the
most basic thing and so we'll keep that
there and now we have a web hook based
trigger this is a really powerful update
for those of you who want to enroll
contacts into workflows using data from
other systems that are being sent out
through web hooks that's all I've got
for you this month be sure to tune in
next month for another product spotlight
video where we will continue
highlighting all the great work
hubspot's product teams are doing to
make HubSpot a better tool for you to
grow better

the type automatically switched to call
based on the title but you can also

something that I really really recommend

## 168. HubSpot's Spring 2026 Spotlight： Scale support

What if you could handle twice the
support volume without doubling your
team? And give customers answers when
they need them, not just when your team
clocks in.
Your customers don't all work in the
same time zone. They might hit issues
outside of your support team's working
hours.
If support only runs when your team is
online, tickets pile up and customers
stay blocked. Say you're a support
manager at a SaaS company.
You log in the next day and find 142
off-hour requests. "How do I export my
report? Where's the archived projects
field? I can't give my client access."
Not hard questions, but they've been
sitting unanswered all weekend, blocking
real work.
Drift Customer Agent in HubSpot works
alongside your team in Help Desk,
handling routine inquiries 24/7.
You start with a free trial and set it
to monitor your inbox after hours. When
emails come in nights and weekends,
Customer Agent reads the ticket, pulls
from your help docs and context, and
drafts responses for your team to
review. Now, the next morning rolls
around. Your team opens Help Desk. 89
drafted responses are waiting. Export
questions, permission issues, feature
locations. Instead of writing from
scratch, they're scanning, tweaking,
sending.
What should have taken until 2:00 p.m.
wraps up by 10:00 a.m.
The agent also knows what not to touch.
30 emails got flagged for human
expertise. Bug reports, billing
questions, a frustrated customer locked
out before a client presentation. After
4 weeks, you're ready to let Customer
Agent work on its own. You start small.
10% of after-hours volume for basic
questions like password resets goes out
automatically.
Everything else still gets reviewed. You
set it to only operate nights and
weekends when your team is offline. The
results hold up, so you expand. 25%,
then 40.
The agent handles routine tickets. Your
team focus on the complex stuff that
actually needs a human.
3 months later, you're solving 25% more
tickets even faster.
Complex issues still get expert
attention. You're supporting twice the
customers with the same team size. Want
to scale support and solve up to 65% of
tickets automatically? Start with
Customer Agent free 28-day trial today.

## 173. INBOUND 2021 - Spotlight Customer CRM Stories

my name's mike hart i'm the global cio
of avis and young one of the challenges
we were faced with our previous crm was
the fact that they didn't speak to one
another but a larger challenge was just
the industry perception within
commercial real estate
of all of our competitors being on
either microsoft dynamics or salesforce
and avis and young is very much thought
of as that challenge or brand within the
industry that we do things differently
we think about data we think about
technology we think about how we go to
market with our various products in a
different fashion hi i'm dave matson ceo
of sandler we consider ourselves a
scaling company which in my mind means
that we're never satisfied with what we
did last year we're always competing
against the you know the large mega
organization so we've got to make sure
that we're always looking for the
smartest way to do things we're always
looking for where the puck is going to
go to in our industry which is
constantly changing but that's why we
went with hubspot because they allowed
us that flexibility as the industry
changes we didn't have to change
platforms so if i look back four months
before we rolled out the hubspot
instance we had about 600 users in north
america of which we only had 23 adoption
and that was of the existing salesforce
platform when you have only 23 of your
revenue producers using a crm it's it's
ultimately a loss it's it's ultimately
something that is not adding value to
the overall organization in the last
four months we've actually already
gotten it up to a 90 adoption rate
amongst those 1600 brokers nobody wakes
up as a leader saying today's the day
that i've got to change a platform but
you know we're at a point where
as you start to look at
all of our offices throughout the world
you needed a single view and we had huge
data gaps actually and we needed to
expand that crm should be your best
friend it should be a silent sales
manager it should be a silent sales
person that's helping you a coach on
your shoulders giving you the
information that you need to run a
business and that's really what hubspot
allowed us to do by giving us the data
so we could make those decisions and we
felt very confident and you know we had
that conviction that we were heading in
the right direction so that's really
what it did for us

## 176. Implementing AI as an Equalizer ｜ Manobyte & HubSpot

what we've always done is we help solve
for customers problems and to help to
get them unstuck so that they can
experience growth. And the reason that
we pivot is because when we see the
future of what needs to happen and the
customers aren't there yet, we're there
to help them feel that need and then get
unstuck and be able to grow um without
those those chains and challenges.

the opportunity really is great and I I
do remember the early days and um we
were able to add value for customers and
I think that's where we are again it's
like we're able now to add value because

a a big thing of what we do is we're

um these agents the ability to really

## 202. Introducing the Agentic Customer Platform ｜ HubSpot

Everyday millions of people ask AI to
write an email and everyday millions of
people rewrite that email. Not because
the grammar is wrong or the tone is off,
but because AI doesn't know that Sarah
and procurement hates long emails or
that the last time you push too hard the
client went dark. AI generates a ton of
output but output isn't outcome. So
what's missing? Context. Your top
performers know which prospects to
prioritize because they've seen the
pattern. They write emails that convert
because they understand your brand and
your audience. They solve
problems fast because they remember what
worked. That's context. The right
information at the right time combined
with the judgement to act on it. Most AI
tools don't have it. They give generic
answers based on the one-size-fits-all
wisdom of the internet. AI knows a lot
about the world but it doesn't know your
world. So what would it look
like if it did? It would know your
business the way your best people do.
Every conversation, every deal,
every lesson learned. That's what
HubSpot is building, an agentic customer
platform and it starts with
context. The context layer is the
foundation where customer understanding
actually lives.
It starts with complete customer data,
not just structured records like
companies, contacts, and deals
>>
>> but unstructured data too. Emails, call
transcripts, chat conversations, support
interactions, the full picture of every
relationship. Then there is the business
context, your brand, your
products, your strategies and more
importantly, not just what happened but
why. Why you escalated that deal. Why
that campaign worked. The rationale
behind decisions that usually live
scattered across email and
inside people's heads. Team context
understands your team's roles and
captures how your people actually work,
their patterns, expertise, and
institutional knowledge that typically
disappears when someone leaves. Industry
intelligence draws on insights from
250,000 plus companies. HubSpot
knows what works for businesses like
yours and that looks different from a
100 person software company versus a
1,000 person manufacturer. And domain
knowledge, 20 years of go-to-market
expertise across marketing,
sales, and service. The action layer is
where work gets done, especially the
kind that piles up. There's always more
prospecting to do, more leads to
qualify, more tickets to answer. That's
just how it is. These agents handle all
of it and because they're pulling from
the context layer,
>>
>> a support agent already knows the
customer's full history. A sales agent
already knows which deals are worth
chasing. It's like onboarding a teammate
who's already been there for years. The
coordination layer is where humans and
agents work together. You set the
boundaries. What agents can handle on
their own, what needs a human. You see
what's happening, why it's happening and
you can step in whenever you want. Your
business doesn't run in one system and
neither should your agents. HubSpot
connects to your broader tech stack
so agents can pull information
from external systems, coordinate with
other AI agents, and take action
wherever it's needed. They become the
connective tissue between your
tools just like your best employees are
today. This isn't AI that runs off and
does its own thing. It works with your
team, not around them. Here's the truth.
AI models are becoming commodities. Any
company can use them. What AI can't
replicate is your customer data, your
business knowledge,
and proven insights from hundreds of
thousands of companies unified
in one place. That's the agentic
customer platform.
AI knows the world, HubSpot knows
your world.

## 205. Lead Validation and Routing with Data Agent

The profile of this customer, they're a
global provider of corporate fund and
private client services and they operate
across multiple business units. For
example, they may offer payroll or they
may offer legal services. The biggest
challenge that they had was their
contact form is used for a
number of reasons. People trying to sell
to them, people who are genuinely
looking for their services, existing
customers who have an issue, and
people looking for jobs. So there were
all sorts of different reasons
that people would contact uh this
organization using the contact form
because of the amount of leads that they
had coming inbound. They
struggled to validate those leads and
then they also struggled to identify how
to route it to the relevant department.
I went very quickly to AI
because actually the rule sets that they
have in place are relatively black and
white and it was quite a timeconsuming
process for an individual to
actually run through. So the the two
main tools that we used are the uh data
agent in HubSpot and it consists
actually of one two 3 4 five different
agents for this process because
not only did we need to identify whether
or not they were a customer and whether
it was a legitimate lead, we then needed
to put them into the relevant segments
and the second one is to use workflows
to actually deploy the solution and then
custom code within the solutions to
leverage the API to be able to
search the database to enable us to find
these existing customers. We found out
actually halfway through this process
that they had an AI governance team that
needed to sign this process off
and they were happy by looking at
HubSpot's description of how that data
is used but the data residency stays
within HubSpot. They were able
to sign that off. The successes that
we've seen have done a number of things
actually. They've led us to understand
the accuracy of the AI, but they've also
led us to understand the inaccuracy of
some of the team decisions that have
been made. So, it's acting as a a
training mechanism for the team to be
able to understand where they're making
mistakes as well. And it's also
helped identify other areas. So, the
business didn't realize they had
challenges. They're now considering
using HubSpot as a ticketing system. Um,
so it's opened up another conversation
and another module within HubSpot that
we can actually start to build for them
as well. So, it's it's
developing work and streamlining their
processes more.

---

Group-wide input, effective onboarding,

with that comes a lot of different

many of our agencies have their own

so, we needed all those different
challenges under one HubSpot instance.
So, to help address this diversity and

we call a steering committee composed of
leaders from several different entities
across the network. So, we had one
person from sales, somebody from
marketing, somebody from legal, somebody
from IT, and we would meet twice a week
leading up to kickoff. Kickoff meaning
that initial onboarding. And then
gradually as the implementation
progressed, um that frequency went down
to about once a month. So, some of the
things that we would talk about in those
meetings were things like life cycle
stages, deal stages, user permissions,
everything that's up here, lead scoring,
what makes a hot lead, what makes a cold
lead. So, we knew we wanted that
group-wide consensus, so that's why this
steering committee made perfect sense.
Us at MNC Saatchi & Saatchi 121, ahead
of that steering committee meeting, we
would send out an agenda, say, "Okay,
this this meeting we're going to focus
on deal stages. So, come with your
thoughts about deal stages, what that
should look like, what the pipeline
should look like. Should we have one
shared pipeline? Should we have
different?" So, we as experts would come

based on best practices, what we already

everybody agree, "Okay, this is what the

we can move on to the next decision. So,
that way we were able to make the
decisions pretty quickly and efficiently
with getting everyone's buy-in as well.
And of course, keeping that paper trail
so that a year later we can go back and

group-wide input with an effective
steering committee. So, make sure to
include the necessary stakeholders. I I
kind of already reviewed that one. Make

agenda ahead of time. So, my tips to
avoid, do not include non-decision
makers. You don't need to waste their
time, they don't need to waste theirs.
Just make sure you have the people who
need to be there in the meeting and
nobody extra. It's It's not needed.
Yielding unclear action items. So,
make sure people know exactly what's
going to happen from that meeting, they
know what the next steps are. And then,
also do not provide too many options
that lead to decision fatigue. Giving
too many options, like let's stay
focused on what we need to talk about,
what we need to cover in this session.
So, my next big component of success is
purposeful onboarding. Close to 50% of
CRM implementations fail, and one of the
most common reasons for this failure is
because low user adoption and lack of
training and support. You need to make
sure that that training piece is is
critical in that onboarding process. So,
we were actually able to onboard our
teams in less than 3 weeks for each
team. We could have just recorded a
webinar and sent that to everybody, but
we wanted to make sure each team had
that personalized onboarding experience,
and that the training sessions addressed
their unique challenges. So, depending
on existing data and integrations, this
would be a typical timeline. Day one, we
would have a kickoff call with them and
just a reminder this is all over Zoom
because like I said, we have many
international teams, so it just made
sense to do it all remotely. So, we'd
have that kickoff call, give an overview
of what this onboarding process would
look like, sometimes even give a review
of what HubSpot could do,
get them excited about the tool, and
then we would give them their first
piece of homework. And so, that was
selecting their contact sheets.
Um so, like I I mentioned before, many
of these teams didn't have an existing
CRM, so their customer and prospect
details are stored in emails and random
spreadsheets, probably not the best not
the best solution there. So, we asked
them to put everything in this template,
and then we would do a contact upload
session together. So, about 2 weeks in,
we would jump on a Zoom together, we
would um have them share their screen,
and we would actually talk them through
the the import process in HubSpot. So,
they had access to HubSpot at that
point. And the reason why we did that uh
there's two reasons. Um one, we wanted
them to get hands-on experience with the
tool so that they could really do the
import process themselves, and also just
for privacy policy reasons, GDPR
purposes, we didn't want them sending
like Excel sheets with private contact
details across email or without password
protection. So, that was our solution
for that. And then a couple days later,
we would do the sales training,
and then a few days later, we would do a
marketing training session. So, an hour
focus just on the marketing tools. After
that last session, we would schedule a
1-month check-in where we would um
review any features that we didn't have
a chance to cover in the initial
training, answer any questions, get
their feedback on the tool. Um so, it
was really great to have that that
check-in. And so, my next piece of
success with this onboarding and
training process was what we call a
HubSpot blueprint. So, this document
acts as a manual for all aspects of the
training and HubSpot setup. So, it's
basically a big PDF that we put together
that was custom to that team's unique
challenges and our our business and our
HubSpot instance. Of course, we we we
told them where to look for HubSpot
support they that many of you already
know, the amazing knowledge base, they
can chat with support online, but also
they had this PDF that they could refer
to with any questions. But, it was a it
was a tangible document. I mean, of
course, most people didn't print it out
cuz it was lots of pages.
Um but, it was a tangible document that
they could look back at any time. So, my
top tips for purposeful training and
onboarding, create that HubSpot
blueprint. We saw a lot of success with
that. Make sure to record the training.
This one might seem obvious, but I
forgot to record the training once and
it's just a really helpful thing to send
in that follow-up. And you might think
people are paying attention and they
probably are, but there's just so much
information that you're covering that
they want to reference that training.
Making sure to prioritize that
follow-up. And then my tips to avoid
introducing an overwhelming amount of
features up front. Make sure to train on
what they need to know first. Like,
what's that first few weeks going to
look like? What are they need to going
to need to use HubSpot for? So, focusing
on that, HubSpot has a lot of
sophisticated features that we could
probably spend hours and hours talking
about. Um but, really focusing on what
they need to know. And then over
estimating a team's CRM or tech
knowledge. So, like I I touched on
before, even just going back to what is
a CRM? Um what is HubSpot? So, just
starting at the basics really lays that
foundation for people. And then giving
ambiguous next steps. Be very clear on
what's going to happen after this last
training session or that last check-in
call. Being super clear. So, next up is
this tailored branding solutions for
diverse teams. So, as I mentioned
before, we're a large global team with
unique needs, different challenges. So,
how are we going to fit all those
different needs it under one roof, under
one HubSpot instance? Our solution to
this was business units. So, this is a
relatively new HubSpot feature. It
allows you to manage contacts, assets,
and settings across multiple brands but
in one HubSpot instance. So, for us this
meant teams were always on brand. So,
each business unit, each team had its
own brand kit. So, we had hex codes in
there, their logo, their assets, and it
was individual to that business unit. Um
it also allowed us to manage
subscriptions on a large scale. So, if
we have one business unit that's sending
marketing emails, and that contact wants
to opt out of those emails, they can opt
out of those emails without opting out
of the whole entire group's marketing
emails, if that's what they want. So, it
gives kind of like that individual
consumer preferences on a large scale.
Helps you declutter assets within your
HubSpot hub, and then also work globally
across multiple markets and
international markets. HubSpot has a
great page all about when you should use
business units, when you should have
separate HubSpot accounts, when
everything can be in one instance
without a business unit. Um but these
are just some highlights, some top tips
that I have. So, adopting business units
when prospects or services may overlap
across brands. As I mentioned before, we
often cross-collaborate different
agencies might work on the same client,
might work on the same project. So,
business units made perfect sense for us
for that. Using business units when the
desire is to connect multiple domains.
So, as I mentioned before, we have
different websites. So, connecting those
domains means that we can have the
HubSpot tracking code on each of those
websites and have individual analytic
page for each of those websites. So, do
not apply business units when you need
separate billing. Though, if you need
separate invoices for whatever reason,
if it truly is like two different
companies, probably business units won't
work for you. And then, do not rely on
business units to partition assets. This
is really where like HubSpot teams and
user permissions come in. So, shared
visibility while complying with data
protection laws. So, this is something
that's been coming up a lot recently. Um
things like GDPR, CCPA, different
privacy laws that we have to comply
with. And being a a global agency, we
have to make sure we're compliant with
all this fun stuff. But, at the same
time, we want that that culture of
transparency. So, how do we do this?
This really comes down to permission
sets. So, how much data is visible into
whom? So, this is an example company
record. Many of you are familiar with
this interface looks like. So, we
configured permissions for our HubSpot
account so that all company data is
visible to all users. So, if it's a
company that's being prospected or an
existing customer,
no matter who owns that relationship,
anyone can see that data. And it's it's
similar to what you would see in
LinkedIn. It's headquarter information,
their website,
but only the owning team can edit or
delete that company.
Um so, that just makes sure that people
aren't just going out on a whim just
deleting or editing companies that they
don't have the relationship with. And
then, on the other side of the company
record is the contacts that work at that
company. So, you can see here that you
can see the the name, the title of that
person, and then who owns that
relationship. You can't click into it.
So, it's not highlighted in blue. So,
everybody can see those high-level
details, but they can't click into it.
So, they can't see private information
like email address, phone number. Only
the agency that owns that relationship
can see those details. And then similar
to deal data, everybody can see that a
deal exists, knows that that's a current
client, but you can't click into it. So,
you can't see like revenue information
unless you own that deal, unless you're
the one managing that deal.
So, my top tips for the shared
visibility while still complying with
these data protection laws, survey your
team leads early to build these
permission sets. Make sure you get their
input on what sales users are going to
need, what marketing users are going to
need, different teams. You can do this
in just a literal survey that you can
send out if you want to get that
feedback. Make sure to outline those
visibility expectations up front with
your users as you're training them. And
then lastly, make sure to make GDPR,
CCPA decisions
early and involve your legal and IT
teams cuz at the end of the day, most of
us are just we're marketing people,
we're HubSpot admins, we're not legal
advice. So, make sure you consult
experts on your on your company for
those type of questions. My tips to
avoid, avoid passing contact sheets
across email without password protection
or just passing them at all. We we
touched on this one earlier with the the
onboarding and training. Do not be
unreceptive to permission set feedback.
For your early users, you're probably
going to have some tweaks to what the
permission set should look like. So, be
receptive to that and make those changes
as needed. And then of course, failure
to consult your legal team. Make sure
you talk to your legal team about all
this stuff. So, we were able to build
the MVP, onboard our teams in less than
6 months. So, it was a pretty fast and
efficient rollout. But now that
everybody's onboarded, it's been a
several months now, how do we maintain
this momentum post rollout? So, this is
where we're getting into all those fun
Sales Hub features, the marketing
features, and really making sure that
the platform is sticky and our users are
coming back to the tool. As I mentioned
before, our main goal with HubSpot was
to accelerate this revenue growth. So,
we do this using deal pipelines, the
shared visibility across dashboards, and
sales reports that help make spotting
these opportunities a lot easier. So,
here's an example pipeline. It's just
the first few stages of our pipeline.
This is all dummy data. But, as you can
see, we have one pipeline, custom deal
names that suit all the entities. We do
have a few um people in senior

um everybody's pipelines and everybody's

And then, just a couple other things I

up-to-date exchange rates.
So, the other thing I'll touch on is how

These are just a few that I'll call out

Contact, deal, and task management,

So, my top tips for maintaining momentum

That's already built out if your teams

Prioritizing integrations. So, the

## 217. Marketing Studio ｜ HubSpot Spotlight Fall 2025

Marketing Studio helps you build multi-
channelannel campaigns from just one
simple idea. Plan, create, and launch
multi-channel campaigns, all using what
HubSpot already knows about your
business. Get a head start with AI
generated campaigns based on any context
you provide. Then collaborate with your
team to customize and execute. Get
creative with intuitive drag and drop to
plan your entire campaign flow with your
team. Generate content quickly, already
integrated into HubSpot's powerful suite
of tools. Your campaign comes in many
views, matching how your team works
best. Collaborate in real time to get
campaigns launched in days, not weeks.
Drop notes, assign tasks, set schedules,
see reports,
all within Marketing Studio. Bring your
vision to life on a creative canvas
built for collaboration.

---
